rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL
functional_target: SOF-LLM — orthogonal-projection-segregated persistent FRAME identity/safety + replaceable SKIN adapter for an LLM

results.reason: kw<2, sem<0.7, func<0.7 — source-domain kayak craft page
sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7 — LoRA decouples safety into orthogonal subspace, identical SOF-LLM frame/skin orthogonality claim
sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7 — OPLoRA double-sided orthogonal projection preserves top-k singular subspace; same architectural skin/frame split
sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7 — SC-LoRA constrains LoRA to orthogonal-to-preserved-knowledge directions; same primitive
sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7 — SAILS explicit safety subspace via SAE with capability preservation; same mechanism
sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7 — DSCA partition into orthogonal semantic subspaces with by-construction isolation; same SOF-LLM split
kw<2, sem<0.7, func<0.7 — aLoRA efficient inference-time adapter swap, not safety/identity orthogonality
kw<2, sem<0.7, func<0.7 — curvature-based post-FT safety restoration, not orthogonality-by-construction prevention

fail_reason: 5 of 8 results exceed both semantic (>=0.7) and functional (>=0.7) thresholds. Multiple 2025-2026 arXiv preprints (2510.09004, 2510.13003v2, 2505.23724v1, 2512.23260, 2604.07965) implement the same core SOF-LLM mechanism: LoRA/adapter updates constrained to an orthogonal complement of a preserved safety/identity subspace by architectural construction. SOF-LLM's projection-enforced two-persistence-layer is not novel; orthogonal-subspace LoRA for safety/knowledge preservation is established prior art across at least 5 distinct effect clusters.
